summary.goal: Fix the data-corruption bug in UpdateScheduleCard where taskId-linked card updates erase inherited field values, and add missing backend test coverage for taskId-based schedule card creation.
summary.diff_summary: Branch feature/2a5f914b-171f-4ccc-a5c0-11f959f3900a (HEAD 8f38248) modifies 7 files: backend/api/daily_schedule.go (formatting), backend/api/tasks.go (ErrTaskHasScheduleCards sentinel, 400 response), backend/main_test.go (TestDeleteTaskWithScheduleCards), backend/store.go (indentation fix, wrap error), backend/store_mock_test.go (scheduleTaskRefs, TaskHasScheduleCards in mock), frontend/src/calendar/CalendarView.vue (restored free-form fields, correct handleSubmit for both paths), frontend/src/calendar/__tests__/CalendarView.spec.ts (daily schedule mock, mutation spies, taskId create test, edit modal tests).

item.request: Fix the data corruption bug in UpdateScheduleCard: when frontend omits title/priority/roomArea/peopleNeeded for taskId-linked card edits, backend unconditionally writes empty/zero values to SQL. Add inheritance logic similar to CreateScheduleCard (resolve from GetTaskByIDForRef for empty input fields).
item.impacted_code: backend/store.go:718-763 — UpdateScheduleCard function body: passes input.Title/Priority/RoomArea/PeopleNeeded directly to tx.UpdateScheduleCard without resolving from referenced task.
item.notes: REPEATED from all 3 review rounds (lead-dev, reviewer 3, reviewer 4). This is the most critical remaining bug. CreateScheduleCard at ~line 654 has the correct pattern: it resolves refTask via GetTaskByIDForRef when taskId is set, and inherits title/priority/roomArea/peopleNeeded when input fields are empty/zero. UpdateScheduleCard needs the same logic before calling tx.UpdateScheduleCard.

item.request: Add backend tests for creating schedule cards with taskId: (1) valid taskId should inherit task fields, (2) invalid taskId should return 400, (3) taskId + explicit field overrides.
item.impacted_code: backend/main_test.go — no tests exist for taskId-based schedule card creation. Existing tests (TestCreateScheduleCard, TestCreateScheduleCardValidation) do not include taskId.
item.notes: Unresolved. No backend test exercises the taskId inheritance logic in CreateScheduleCard. The mock store already supports TaskID via scheduleTaskRefs and scheduleCards map (it stores cardRow.TaskID if non-empty). Test setup possible with CreateScheduleCardInput{TaskId: "task-1", ...}.

item.request: TestScheduleCardWriteReflectsInDailySchedule should verify taskId field appears in daily-schedule response after creating a taskId-linked card. Add a separate test for cards without taskId returning null.
item.impacted_code: backend/main_test.go:2101 — TestScheduleCardWriteReflectsInDailySchedule does not assert taskId in the response.
item.notes: Unresolved. The test creates a card without taskId and checks Title/Priority in the daily-schedule response but never checks taskId. Should add assertions that taskId is null for this case, plus a separate variant with taskId set.

item.request: Restore free-form form fields for edit mode when selectedTask is null (unreferenced cards). Populate from existing card values, use form values in handleSubmit instead of hardcoded defaults.
item.impacted_code: frontend/src/calendar/CalendarView.vue — previously critical bug where edit path for unreferenced cards showed task selector and sent empty/dummy data.
item.notes: ALREADY FIXED. Current HEAD adds formTitle/formPriority/formPeopleNeeded/formRoomArea refs, openEdit populates them for unreferenced cards, handleSubmit uses form values in the else branch.

item.request: Define sentinel error ErrTaskHasScheduleCards and use errors.Is check in DeleteTask handler to return 400 instead of 500.
item.impacted_code: backend/api/tasks.go:284 — DeleteTask handler; backend/store.go:597 — DeleteTask error message.
item.notes: ALREADY FIXED. ErrTaskHasScheduleCards defined in api/tasks.go at line 28. Handler at line 293 checks errors.Is(err, ErrTaskHasScheduleCards) and returns huma.Error400BadRequest. Store wraps the error correctly with %w.

item.request: Fix inconsistent indentation in store.go (var taskIDPtr *string).
item.notes: ALREADY FIXED. Indentation corrected in current HEAD.

item.request: Frontend tests: verify create mutations include taskId in request body, add edit modal tests for referenced and unreferenced cards.
item.impacted_code: frontend/src/calendar/__tests__/CalendarView.spec.ts — previously no capturing mock assertions or edit modal tests.
item.notes: ALREADY FIXED. Current HEAD has createMutateAsync/updateMutateAsync capturing spies, test "sends taskId in create mutation body", and "Edit Modal" describe block with referenced and unreferenced card tests.

item.request: Add backend test for TestDeleteTaskWithScheduleCards (task delete protection).
item.impacted_code: backend/main_test.go — no test existed for deleting a task that has referencing schedule cards.
item.notes: ALREADY FIXED. TestDeleteTaskWithScheduleCards added at line 1122. Also mock store DeleteTask checks TaskHasScheduleCards before deleting.

code_area.reason: Primary fix target: UpdateScheduleCard (~line 718) needs inheritance logic identical to CreateScheduleCard (~line 654). Must resolve from GetTaskByIDForRef when TaskId is non-empty and input fields are empty/zero before calling tx.UpdateScheduleCard.

Defines CreateScheduleCardInput struct (line 16-24) and TaskCard response struct (line 66+) with TaskId field. Input type already has TaskId string. Response already returns TaskId. No schema changes needed.

Missing tests: TestCreateScheduleCardWithValidTaskId, TestCreateScheduleCardWithInvalidTaskId, TestCreateScheduleCardWithTaskIdAndOverrides. Also need to extend TestScheduleCardWriteReflectsInDailySchedule to verify taskId in response.

Mock store already supports TaskID in CreateScheduleCard (stores cardRow.TaskID in scheduleTaskRefs). No changes needed for mock. However, mockUpdateScheduleCard may also need the same inheritance fix if it's called in tests.
Already fixed in current HEAD. Verify no further changes needed. The free-form fields, openEdit path, and handleSubmit are correct.
Already has taskId create mutation test and edit modal tests. Verify assertions are sufficient.

planner_constraints.constraint: The backend uses a mock store (mockStore) for unit tests. The UpdateScheduleCard inheritance fix must also be applied to mockUpdateScheduleCard if it exists, otherwise mock tests will diverge from real behavior.

The CreateScheduleCardInput struct is shared between create and update paths. Both use the same api.CreateScheduleCardInput type. This is fine — the inheritance logic in UpdateScheduleCard should use the same GetTaskByIDForRef query.

Frontend handleSubmit for taskId-linked card updates sends only taskId, scheduledDate, and assignedTo in the PUT body — intentionally omitting title/priority/roomArea/peopleNeeded. The backend fix must NOT require frontend changes.
All backend tests use httptest (integration-style) against mockStore, not PostgreSQL. Tests for taskId-based creation must work with existing mock infrastructure that already supports TaskID.
The precommit verification script (scripts/precommit-run) must pass after changes.

assumptions.assumption: The UpdateScheduleCard fix is the only remaining blocking issue. All other feedback items from previous review rounds have been addressed in the current HEAD.
The mock store's CreateScheduleCard already handles TaskId correctly (stores it in scheduleTaskRefs). The mock UpdateScheduleCard may need updating to match PgStore behavior after the fix.
Frontend tests that assert on HTML content (e.g., "From backlog") are sufficient for verifying the edit modal display modes.
Backend tests will use the mock store (newMockStore) which has pre-seeded tasks (task-1, task-2) and schedule cards available for test setup.

open_questions.question: Does mockStore.UpdateScheduleCard currently handle TaskID? If not, does it need to be updated to match PgStore behavior (storing the taskId in scheduleTaskRefs) to support taskId-linked update tests?

Should UpdateScheduleCard always query the referenced task (like CreateScheduleCard does), or should it also fall back to the existing DB values when input fields are empty/zero? The reviewer suggests task inheritance, but existing DB values could also be a valid fallback for fields the user doesn't want to change.
Does TestUpdateScheduleCard need a varaint that tests taskId-linked card update to prevent regression?

recommended_validation.step: After adding inheritance logic to UpdateScheduleCard: (1) PgStore: write a test that creates a card with taskId, then updates it with only scheduledDate and assignedTo (omitting title/priority/roomArea/peopleNeeded), then asserts the inherited fields are preserved. (2) Mock store: mirror the same behavior. (3) Run go test ./backend/... to verify no regressions. (4) Run frontend vitest for CalendarView.spec.ts. (5) Run scripts/precommit-run for full verification.
After adding backend taskId-based creation tests: Run specific tests for valid taskId, invalid taskId, taskId+overrides, and daily-schedule reflection with taskId.
